query:
You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines repository.
The codebase is at version 1.7 (commit: d47e9f32).

## Issue to Resolve

`SharedFlow.toList(mutableList)`: difference behavior between 1.6.4 vs 1.7.0-Beta
Sometimes, I use this method to write simple test on SharedFlow.
Version 1.6.4 works well.
Since 1.7.0-Beta, this method is deprecated and the implementation seem be wrong

```kotlin
val acc = MutableList()
val job = scope.launch(start = Undispatched) {
  sharedFlow.toList(acc)
}

// later...

job.cancel()
assertContentEquals(
 expected = ...,
 actual = acc
)
```

IMO, the correct impl. is `SharedFlow.toList(acc) = (this as Flow).toList(acc)`

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

+1 to this issue and proposed solution. Seems this commit changed the behavior: https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/commit/f981a1d00a9d75169321f5ef63fed16622c3e44f

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.text:
Now I can see the issue! Looking at the `toList` function on lines 147-148:

```kotlin
public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toList(destination: MutableList<T> = ArrayList()): List<T> =
    (this as Flow<T>).toList()
```

The `destination` parameter is being ignored! The implementation calls `(this as Flow<T>).toList()` without passing the `destination` parameter, so it always creates a new ArrayList instead of filling the provided one.

The same issue exists in `toSet` on lines 159-160:

```kotlin
public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toSet(destination: MutableSet<T> = LinkedHashSet()): Set<T> =
    (this as Flow<T>).toSet()
```

Let me fix both issues:

function.name: edit
function.arguments: {"file_path": "/project/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt", "old_string": "public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toList(destination: MutableList<T> = ArrayList()): List<T> =\n    (this as Flow<T>).toList()", "new_string": "public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toList(destination: MutableList<T> = ArrayList()): List<T> =\n    (this as Flow<T>).toList(destination)"}

function.arguments: {"file_path": "/project/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt", "old_string": "public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toSet(destination: MutableSet<T> = LinkedHashSet()): Set<T> =\n    (this as Flow<T>).toSet()", "new_string": "public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toSet(destination: MutableSet<T> = LinkedHashSet()): Set<T> =\n    (this as Flow<T>).toSet(destination)"}

content.text: Let me try using sed to make the changes:

function.name: run_terminal_cmd
function.arguments: {"command": "sed -i 's/(this as Flow<T>).toList()/(this as Flow<T>).toList(destination)/g' /project/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt", "explanation": "Fix toList to pass destination parameter"}

content.text: The fix has been successfully applied. Let me verify the changes one more time to confirm:

conversation_history.name: grep
conversation_history.content:
kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt:148:    (this as Flow<T>).toList(destination)
--
kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt:160:    (this as Flow<T>).toSet(destination)
[Showing results with pagination = limit: 100, offset: 0]

content.text:
The fix has been successfully applied. Let me summarize what was done:

## Summary

### Root Cause
The `SharedFlow.toList(destination)` and `SharedFlow.toSet(destination)` functions in `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt` were ignoring their `destination` parameters. 

**Before (buggy):**
```kotlin
public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toList(destination: MutableList<T> = ArrayList()): List<T> =
    (this as Flow<T>).toList()  // destination parameter ignored!

public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toSet(destination: MutableSet<T> = LinkedHashSet()): Set<T> =
    (this as Flow<T>).toSet()  // destination parameter ignored!
```

The implementation was calling `Flow.toList()` and `Flow.toSet()` without passing the `destination` parameter, so it always created new collections instead of filling the user-provided ones.

### The Fix
**After (fixed):**
```kotlin
public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toList(destination: MutableList<T> = ArrayList()): List<T> =
    (this as Flow<T>).toList(destination)  // now passes destination

public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toSet(destination: MutableSet<T> = LinkedHashSet()): Set<T> =
    (this as Flow<T>).toSet(destination)  // now passes destination
```

This ensures that when users call `sharedFlow.toList(acc)` with a pre-existing mutable list, the elements will be added to that list as expected, rather than being added to a new list that gets discarded. This matches the behavior from version 1.6.4 and implements exactly what the issue reporter suggested: `SharedFlow.toList(acc) = (this as Flow).toList(acc)`.
